name: Nintendo Entertainment System
description: The console that saved the video game industry after the 1983 crash. Changed gaming forever.

name: Super Nintendo Entertainment System
description: 16-bit powerhouse with Mode 7 graphics. Home to some of the greatest games ever made.

name: Sega Genesis Model 1
description: Blast Processing! The Genesis brought arcade-quality games home. Rivals the SNES.

name: Sega Genesis Model 2
description: Smaller and lighter revision of the Genesis. Same great library at a lower price.

name: Nintendo 64
description: The N64 introduced 3D gaming to a generation. Four controller ports for multiplayer.

name: Original PlayStation
description: Sony's entry into gaming changed everything. CD-based games and 3D graphics.

name: Sega Saturn
description: The Saturn had incredible 2D sprite performance. Underrated but beloved by collectors.

name: Atari 2600
description: The granddaddy of home gaming. Where it all began for millions of gamers.

name: Game Boy Original
description: The grey brick that conquered portable gaming. Nearly indestructible.

name: Game Boy Color
description: Added color to the Game Boy lineup while keeping full backward compatibility.

name: Game Boy Advance
description: The GBA packed SNES-level power into a portable. Incredible library of games.

name: Sega Game Gear
description: Sega's answer to the Game Boy with a full-color backlit screen. Battery hungry!

name: Neo Geo Pocket Color
description: SNK's little gem with a satisfying clicky joystick. Rare and collectable.

name: TurboGrafx-16
description: The forgotten console with a cult following. CD add-on made it a powerhouse.

name: Sega Master System
description: The NES's main competitor outside North America. Great library of its own.
